text: Continuum. Agents that remember after they die. When a process crashes mid-mission, most agents forget everything. Continuum does not.
Production agents write constantly, spawn autonomously, and fail. If memory lives only in RAM, the workflow dies with the process.
Continuum stores the mission cursor, event log, and semantic embeddings in CockroachDB on AWS. Amazon Bedrock Titan creates the vectors. Cursor connects through the CockroachDB Cloud MCP server.
Watch the jury proof. We start a real mission, then simulate a crash after step two. Steps three and four stay pending in CockroachDB.
The timeline is not a mock. It is durable task state. Semantic recall pulls related memories from the same database using distributed vector search.
Now we resume. Continuum reads the unfinished cursor and continues from the next pending step. No human re-briefing. No lost context.
The mission completes. Closing memories are written back with Bedrock embeddings, still queryable beside the transactional state.
Continuum. CockroachDB for agentic memory. Bedrock for embeddings. Built so agents survive failure. Memory is the product.
